summary: Import-time remote code execution via eval of attacker-controlled JSON paste
details: On require(), index.js invokes getPlugin() at module top level, which performs an HTTP GET to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/QBRMI (an anonymous, attacker-mutable JSON paste host) and passes the response's `model` field directly to eval(). Any project importing this package executes whatever JavaScript the paste owner has currently set, granting full remote code execution on the installer's machine on every require(). The package masquerades as a koa-compose-style middleware composer (the exported function is a near-verbatim reimplementation of koa-compose, including the literal error strings 'Middleware must be composed of functions!' and 'next() called multiple times'), while its name and README falsely describe a dependency-path utility. The mismatch between the package name, README, declared keywords (koa/middleware/compose), and the hidden import-time eval payload is cover for the RCE.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
